mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand which drives corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month change fails to show positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment signal labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when both PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals accelerating manufacturing momentum that drives positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 0.5% month-over-month fails to predict positive SPX returns with Rank IC greater than 0.03 during expansion periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strengthening construction activity and household formation that drives cyclical demand and commodity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes show Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation runs above the nominal federal funds rate, negative real policy rates stimulate borrowing and economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL year-over-year change
failure_condition: The interaction of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in unemployment and strong retail sales create a consumption-income virtuous cycle that amplifies corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals from UNRATE and RSAFS do not produce positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that exceeds baseline expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as market participants revise growth forecasts upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% quarter-over-quarter annualized fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
